label: USR_03 (Finance Analyst)
description: Compromised via phishing initial access. This user account is the attack origin.

label: USR_01 (HR User)
description: Standard user session. No anomalies detected.

label: USR_02 (Operations User)
description: Standard user session. No anomalies detected.

label: USR_04 (Legal User)
description: Standard user session. No anomalies detected.

label: WST_02 (Finance-PC)
description: Vulnerable to simulated local privilege escalation finding LPE-DEMO-001. Contains exposed service account credentials in memory and creates a path to SVC_01.

label: WST_01 (HR-Laptop)
description: Endpoint protected by EDR. Clean state.

label: WST_03 (IT-Admin-PC)
description: Isolated admin workstation with restricted access. No suspicious activity detected.

label: SVC_01 (Backup-Service)
description: Service account with excessive privileges. Exposed credentials from WST_02 allow attacker movement toward internal servers.

label: SVC_02 (Monitoring-Service)
description: Service account used for monitoring and reporting tasks. No suspicious privilege abuse detected.

label: SRV_01 (SQL Server 01)
description: Internal database server containing sensitive backups. It is part of the active attack path toward DC_01.

label: SRV_02 (Web Server 02)
description: DMZ web server. Patched, monitored, and not part of the active attack path.

label: DB_01 (Backup Database)
description: Database containing sensitive backup records. At risk because SRV_01 is reachable through the active attack path.

label: DC_01 (Domain Controller)
description: Primary Domain Controller. Critical target for domain privilege escalation and full environment compromise.

label: SIEM_01 (SOCSimulator Feed)
description: SOCSimulator-inspired monitoring source used to generate demo SOC logs, MITRE-mapped events, and curated attack path signals.
